zh.St. Giles, Cripplegate, London, England: 英國英格蘭倫敦可利普萊加特
zh.lifeSummary: 當George Anthony Stoddard於1578年3月4日出生在英國英格蘭倫敦聖 基路士 維特 可利普萊加特時，他的父親Martyn Stoddard28歲，母親Isabel Eden25歲。他於大約1600年與Maria Bryant在英國倫敦結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1626年在英國肯特享年49歲。

nameMeanings.name: Stoddard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name for someone who looked after a stud of horses, from Middle English and Older Scots stod(e), stud(e) ‘establishment where horses were bred’, ‘herd of stallions or mares’ + herd(e) ‘herdsman’.

English: variant of Stothard, an occupational name for a keeper of cattle or horses, from Middle English stot ‘steer, bullock’ or ‘horse’ + herd(e) ‘herdsman’. The name was probably confused with Stodeherd ‘keeper of stud-horses’ (see above).

History: The Stoddard family of Boston, MA, was introduced by Anthony Stoddard (1600–1686), who settled there in 1639. Solomon Stoddard (1643–1728/9) was a prominent Congregational clergyman in MA, the grandfather of Jonathan Edwards, and progenitor of many noted descendants.

variants.name: Stodden
meanings: perhaps occasionally a habitational name from Studding's Farm in Herstmonceux (Sussex), probably named with Old English stōd ‘stud, herd of horses’ + denn ‘pasture’. Compare Stoddard .

variants.name: Stoddart
meanings: English (Durham and Cumberland) and Scottish (Midlothian and Lanarkshire): variant of   Stoddard  .

nameMeanings.name: George
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

nameMeanings.name: Anthony
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual English form of the old Roman family name Antonius, which is of uncertain (probably Etruscan) origin. The spelling with -th- (not normally reflected in the pronunciation) represents a learned but erroneous attempt to associate it with Greek anthos ‘flower’. In the post-classical period it was a common name, borne by various early saints, most notably a 3rd-century Egyptian hermit monk, who is regarded as the founder of Christian monasticism.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When George Anthony Stoddard was born on 4 March 1578, in St Giles without Cripplegate, London, England, United Kingdom, his father, Martyn Stoddard, was 28 and his mother, Isabel Eden, was 25. He married Maria Bryant about 1600, in London, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died about 1626, in Kent, England, at the age of 49.

ko.St Giles without Cripplegate, London, En: 영국 잉글랜드 런던 스트 자일스 위토우트 크리플레가트

ko.lifeSummary: George Anthony Stoddard(이)가 1578년 3월 4일 영국 잉글랜드 런던 스트 자일스 위토우트 크리플레가트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Martyn Stoddard(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabel Eden(은)는 25세였다. 그는 대략 1600년 영국 런던에서 Maria Bryant(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1626년 영국 켄트에서 49세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда George Anthony Stoddard родился 4 марта 1578 г. в местности ст джилесь витоут крипплегэит, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Martyn Stoddard было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel Eden, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Maria Bryant около 1600 г. в местности Лондон, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер около 1626 г. в местности кэнт, Англия в возрасте 49 лет.
